title: ceratocapnos claviculata subsp. claviculata
intro: Ceratocapnos claviculata subsp. claviculata, also known as the red fumitory or devil's fiddle, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Papaveraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ceratocapnos claviculata subsp. claviculata include its potential to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
